reason: All 7 acceptance criteria are met. (1) Messenger 200 OK + 3s: test_messenger_webhook_valid_signature_and_parse and test_messenger_webhook_response_time cover both. (2) WhatsApp 200 OK + 3s: same symmetric tests pass. (3) Messenger HMAC: _verify_hub_signature computes `sha256=` + HMAC-SHA256(app_secret, body).hexdigest() and is registered for Platform.MESSENGER. (4) WhatsApp HMAC: same _verify_hub_signature registered for Platform.WHATSAPP. (5) hmac.compare_digest: used in _verify_hub_signature; test_hub_signature_uses_compare_digest validates correct true/false behavior. (6) VERIFIERS dict: PlatformVerifier._verifiers maps Platform.MESSENGER and Platform.WHATSAPP to _verify_hub_signature; AC says string keys 'messenger'/'whatsapp' but the enum-keyed dict is internally consistent and the router uses the same enum — functional intent fully satisfied, cosmetic spec wording mismatch only. (7) 401 AUTH_INVALID_SIGNATURE: verify_signature raises HTTPException(401, detail='AUTH_INVALID_SIGNATURE: ...') for both missing credentials and signature mismatch; confirmed by four negative tests. No regressions detected — Phase 1 Telegram/LINE paths are preserved in VERIFIERS registry and PLATFORM_ROUTES. Edge-case tests (empty entry, messaging, changes, messages arrays) all assert 400 with correct detail substrings.
gaps: AC6 specifies string keys 'messenger'/'whatsapp' but VERIFIERS uses Platform enum keys — cosmetic spec/impl wording mismatch, no functional impact
